top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: The big global headline today is the WHO declaring the Ebola outbreak in DR Congo and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern - a rare and serious step. Elsewhere, Israel killed Hamas's military chief Izz al-Din al-Haddad in Gaza, Russia continued punishing missile strikes on Ukrainian cities after a brief truce, and the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing wrapped up signalling a fragile thaw in trade tensions. On a lighter note, Bulgaria pulled off a record-margin win at Eurovision.

stories.headline: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DR Congo and Uganda a global health emergency
stories.summary: The World Health Organization on May 17 declared the Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak in DR Congo and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern. Eight confirmed cases, 246 suspected cases and around 80 suspected deaths have been reported, with two confirmed cases (one fatal) in Uganda's capital Kampala. There are currently no approved vaccines or therapeutics for the Bundibugyo strain.

stories.headline: Israel kills Hamas military chief Izz al-Din al-Haddad in Gaza City strike
stories.summary: Israel confirmed on May 16 that an airstrike in western Gaza City killed Izz al-Din al-Haddad, head of Hamas's military wing and one of the last surviving architects of the October 7 attacks. Hamas confirmed his death along with that of his wife and daughter, calling the loss 'profound'. Seven people were killed and dozens injured at the strike sites, according to Palestinian medical officials.

stories.headline: Russia hammers Kyiv with overnight missiles and drones, death toll hits 24
stories.summary: Russia launched a massive combined attack with hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles on Kyiv overnight on May 13-14, collapsing a nine-storey residential block in Darnytsia. At least 24 people were killed, including three children, and 48 more injured - the deadliest strike on the capital in months. Zelenskyy ordered the military to prepare a response and Ukraine struck Russian gas facilities 1,500 km from the border in retaliation.

stories.headline: Trump-Xi summit in Beijing wraps with tariff truce expected to be extended
stories.summary: Presidents Trump and Xi concluded a two-day summit in Beijing on May 15, signalling support for stable relations and an extension of the one-year trade truce reached last October. China is expected to ramp up purchases of US soybeans, beef and Boeing jets, while US tariffs on Chinese goods remain elevated at an average of 47.5 percent. Xi warned that mishandling Taiwan could put the relationship 'in great jeopardy'.

stories.headline: Bulgaria wins Eurovision 2026 with record-margin victory
stories.summary: Bulgarian pop star Dara won the Eurovision Song Contest in Vienna on May 16 with the song 'Bangaranga', finishing a record 173 points ahead of Israel's Noam Bettan - the largest winning margin in the contest's history. Israel finished second despite boycott protests, and the UK came last. Next year's contest will be held in Bulgaria.

stories.headline: Trump renews bomb threat against Iran as US-Iran peace memo stalls
stories.summary: President Trump on May 16 again threatened renewed strikes on Iran while pushing for a 14-point one-page memo brokered by Pakistan that would end the war, declare a 30-day negotiating window, and impose a moratorium on Iran's nuclear enrichment. Tehran continues to favour a staggered, phased approach. The ceasefire remains, in Trump's words, 'on massive life support'.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: Singapore's biggest local story tonight is the police revealing footage of an AI deepfake Zoom scam that mimicked PM Lawrence Wong and cost one victim US$3.8 million. Expect thundery showers across the north and west into the evening. Beyond that, freshly released COE prices show Cat A and Cat B premiums above S$124,000, and the CDA is still tracking two residents quarantined after the MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak.

stories.headline: Police detail AI deepfake Zoom scam mimicking PM Wong; victim lost S$4.9 million
stories.summary: Singapore Police on May 17 released details of a sophisticated scam in which fraudsters used a deepfake AI-generated Zoom call impersonating PM Lawrence Wong and senior officials. Victims received WhatsApp messages from someone posing as the cabinet secretary inviting them to a 'meeting' on funding linked to the Strait of Hormuz crisis. One victim alone lost at least S$4.9 million (US$3.8 million).

stories.headline: Thundery showers expected across northern and western Singapore
stories.summary: The Meteorological Service Singapore forecasts thundery showers mainly over northern and western parts of the island in the late afternoon and evening of May 17. The wider fortnightly outlook keeps short-duration thundery showers on most afternoons. PUB has urged residents to subscribe to flash flood alerts via Telegram and SMS.

stories.headline: CDA: two Singapore residents from MV Hondius cruise still in quarantine at NCID
stories.summary: The Communicable Diseases Agency confirmed that two Singapore residents who had been onboard the hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius remain isolated at the National Centre for Infectious Diseases. Both are awaiting test results; if negative, they will face a 30-day quarantine plus 45 days of phone surveillance. CDA says public risk in Singapore remains low.

stories.headline: COE prices: Cat A closes at S$124,790, Cat B at S$126,236 in May round one
stories.summary: LTA's first Certificate of Entitlement bidding exercise of May 2026 closed with Cat A (smaller cars) at S$124,790 and Cat B (larger and luxury cars) at S$126,236. Cat C (commercial vehicles) closed at S$87,479, Cat D (motorcycles) at S$9,452, and Open Cat E at S$127,700. The May-July quota is up 1 percent on the previous quarter.

stories.headline: Carbon tax hike could lift HDB utility bills by about S$3 a month
stories.summary: Singapore's planned carbon tax increase from S$25 to S$45 per tonne is expected to push monthly utility bills for a typical four-room HDB flat up by about S$3 by 2030, according to government estimates. To cushion the impact, the government is paying out up to S$380 a year in U-Save rebates, with more than 950,000 HDB households receiving payments in January.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: All three active theatres look ugly tonight. Russia is back to mass overnight strikes on Kyiv after its US-brokered truce collapsed, Israel just decapitated Hamas's military wing in Gaza, and the US-Iran ceasefire is hanging on talks mediated by Pakistan. Putin keeps hinting the war is 'coming to an end' while bombing apartment blocks; Trump keeps threatening Iran while pushing a peace memo. Watch the ceasefire deadline this week.

theatres.name: Russia-Ukraine War
theatres.status: Active - US-brokered 3-day truce expired, mass strikes resumed

timeline.event: Russia launches full-scale invasion of Ukraine.
Russia launches a record 8,000+ drones at Ukraine, highest monthly total since the war began.
Russia and Ukraine declare competing ceasefires; both immediately accuse each other of violations.
US brokers a 3-day ceasefire and 1,000-prisoner swap, running May 9-11.
Putin publicly suggests the war is 'coming to an end' and offers to meet Zelenskyy in a third country.
Truce expires; Russia fires more than 200 drones overnight at Ukraine. Zelenskyy says Russia has no intention of ending the war.
Russia launches at least 800 drones in waves; combined strike on Kyiv begins in daytime.
Russian missile and drone barrage on Kyiv kills at least 24, including 3 children; nine-storey apartment block collapses in Darnytsia.
Ukraine strikes Russian gas facilities in Orenburg region, more than 1,500 km from the border, in retaliation.
Ukraine claims to have intercepted six Russian Kinzhal hypersonic missiles.
Fighting continues - Ukraine reports 234 combat engagements, 95 Russian airstrikes and 300 guided bombs in the past 24 hours.

theatres.name: US/Israel-Iran (Strait of Hormuz)
theatres.status: Active - ceasefire shaky, US-Iran negotiations via Pakistan ongoing

timeline.event: US and Israel launch joint airstrikes on Iran, killing Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and several officials. War begins.
US-Iran ceasefire announced; Iran's foreign minister declares Strait of Hormuz open. Oil prices fall 11% in one day.
Strait of Hormuz tensions reignite; US and Iranian forces exchange attacks on commercial shipping.
Iran attacks UAE; US sinks 7 small Iranian boats; commercial vessels attacked in the strait.
US and Iran begin work on a one-page, 14-point memo via Pakistani mediators to end the war.
First Chinese oil tanker attacked in the Strait of Hormuz as the conflict widens.
Iran seizes a tanker in the Gulf of Oman; US 'disables' two Iranian ships.
Trump says the ceasefire is on 'massive life support' after rejecting Tehran's counter-proposal.
Trump threatens new strikes if Iran doesn't accept a deal; both sides still talking via Pakistan.
US naval blockade of Iranian ports remains in place; ceasefire deadline looms mid-week.

theatres.name: Israel-Gaza / Lebanon
theatres.status: Active - Israel kills Hamas military chief; Lebanon truce extended

timeline.event: Hamas-led attacks on Israel kill ~1,200 and take ~250 hostages, triggering the Gaza war.
Mohammed Sinwar killed; Izz al-Din al-Haddad takes over Hamas's military wing.
Israeli strike in western Gaza City kills Haddad - last surviving Oct 7 architect - alongside his wife and daughter. Seven killed in total.
Israel-Lebanon truce extended for a further 45 days.
IDF and Hamas both confirm Haddad's death; Hamas calls the loss 'profound'.
Total death toll from Israeli strikes in Lebanon since war began passes 2,800; over 1 million displaced.

hantavirus.label: Hantavirus Outbreak
hantavirus.ai_summary: The MV Hondius outbreak has stabilised at 8 confirmed cases, 2 suspected and 3 deaths, but the geographic footprint keeps widening - former passengers are now hospitalised or quarantined in at least 12 countries. WHO calls the epidemic risk low because the Andes virus needs close, sustained contact to spread, but the case fatality rate for past Andes outbreaks runs 20-40%. Singapore's two cases remain in isolation at NCID.

stories.headline: MV Hondius outbreak: 8 confirmed cases, 3 deaths across 12 countries
stories.summary: As of mid-May, the WHO and ECDC report 8 confirmed and 2 suspected Andes hantavirus cases from the Dutch cruise ship MV Hondius, with three deaths (two confirmed Andes virus). Former passengers are hospitalised or quarantined in Australia, Canada, France, Germany, the Netherlands, Saint Helena, Singapore, South Africa, Spain, Switzerland, Turkey, and the United States.

stories.headline: Singapore's two MV Hondius passengers still in quarantine at NCID
stories.summary: The CDA continues to monitor two Singapore residents who were aboard the MV Hondius. Both are isolated at the National Centre for Infectious Diseases pending test results. If negative, they face 30 days of quarantine plus 45 days of phone surveillance. CDA reiterates that public risk in Singapore is low.

stories.headline: Andes virus: the only hantavirus known to spread between humans
stories.summary: Unlike other hantaviruses, the Andes virus can transmit between people via close, sustained contact and may be airborne. Past Andes outbreaks have had case fatality rates of 20-40 percent, which is why WHO has flagged the outbreak even though it has not yet declared a formal emergency response level. WHO has shipped 2,500 diagnostic kits from Argentina to labs in five countries.

stories.headline: WHO's response to the MV Hondius cluster
stories.summary: WHO Director-General Tedros has signalled travel to Tenerife to oversee disembarkment efforts, with repatriation flights planned. WHO emphasises the epidemic risk is low for the general public, given the virus's reliance on close, sustained contact.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: Two themes dominate this cycle: a maxed-out Cisco SD-WAN zero-day with a federal patch deadline tonight, and the first confirmed real-world use of AI to build a zero-day exploit. CISA added Cisco's CVE-2026-20182 (CVSS 10.0) to its KEV list and ordered agencies to patch by May 17, while Google says it caught hackers using an AI model to generate a 2FA-bypass zero-day. Microsoft's May Patch Tuesday closed 120 flaws with no zero-days; Fortinet, SAP, and Linux all shipped critical fixes.

stories.headline: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN authentication bypass actively exploited (CVE-2026-20182)
stories.summary: Cisco warns that a critical authentication bypass in Catalyst SD-WAN Controller is being exploited in zero-day attacks, letting remote attackers log in as a high-privileged internal user and manipulate SD-WAN fabric configuration. CISA added it to the KEV catalog and ordered federal agencies to patch by today, May 17. Upgrade is the only remediation - no workaround exists.

stories.headline: Google: hackers used AI to build first known zero-day 2FA bypass exploit
stories.summary: Google's Threat Intelligence Group says it identified a threat actor using an AI model to discover and write a zero-day exploit that bypasses two-factor authentication. The criminal group was reportedly preparing a mass-exploitation event before Google's counter-discovery neutralised the campaign. It is the first confirmed real-world case of AI-assisted exploit generation.

stories.headline: Microsoft May Patch Tuesday fixes 120 flaws, no zero-days disclosed
stories.summary: Microsoft's May 2026 Patch Tuesday released on May 12 fixes 120 vulnerabilities across Windows, Office, Azure and developer tools, with no actively exploited zero-days this cycle. Admins are urged to prioritise remote-code-execution and elevation-of-privilege flaws, particularly in Windows Server.

stories.headline: Linux kernel privilege-escalation flaw 'Fragnasia' added to CISA KEV
stories.summary: Linux distros are rolling out patches for a high-severity kernel local privilege escalation tracked as CVE-2026-31431 (nicknamed 'Fragnasia') that lets unprivileged local users become root. CISA has added the bug to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalogue based on evidence of active exploitation.

stories.headline: Fortinet patches critical FortiSandbox auth bypass (CVE-2026-26083)
stories.summary: Fortinet's May 12 advisory closes five vulnerabilities across FortiAP, FortiOS and FortiSandbox, including CVE-2026-26083, an unauthenticated missing-authorisation flaw in FortiSandbox that exposes restricted GUI functionality and sandbox analysis data. A second critical flaw (CVE-2026-44277, CVSS 9.1) affects FortiAuthenticator.

stories.headline: OpenAI confirms breach in TanStack supply-chain attack
stories.summary: OpenAI says two employee devices were compromised in the recent TanStack supply-chain attack, which poisoned hundreds of npm and PyPI packages. The company has rotated its code-signing certificates as a precaution. The attack is being treated as one of the largest npm/PyPI compromises this year.

stories.headline: Windows BitLocker 'YellowKey' zero-day exposes encrypted drives
stories.summary: An anonymous researcher disclosed two Windows zero-days, codenamed YellowKey (a BitLocker bypass in the Windows Recovery Environment) and GreenPlasma (CTFMON privilege escalation). YellowKey affects Windows 11 and Windows Server 2022/2025 and is described as functioning 'like a backdoor' to encrypted drives. A proof of concept has been released.
